name: Honolulu Museum of Art
tagline: Hawaii's largest art museum — 50,000 works spanning 5,000 years, housed in a stunning 1927 building with open-air courtyards.
parking: Street parking on Beretania St or paid lots nearby. Limited museum lot
bestTime: First Friday of the month — free admission, live music, food trucks, and a festive atmosphere. Weekday mornings are quietest
kamaaina: Free admission for Hawaii residents with valid ID. General admission $20
talkStory: Founded by Anna Rice Cooke in 1927, this museum is a hidden gem that most visitors skip. The building itself is gorgeous — a series of galleries connected by open-air courtyards with koi ponds and tropical gardens. The collection spans Asian, European, American, Pacific Island, and Hawaiian art. Highlights include a world-class collection of Japanese woodblock prints, a Monet, a Van Gogh, and rotating contemporary exhibitions. The museum cafe is excellent for lunch. Also operates Shangri La (Doris Duke's Islamic art estate) — book that separately.
